2603.21988.title: TREX: Trajectory Explanations for Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
2603.21988.paper_summary:
Reinforcement Learning (RL) has demonstrated its ability to solve complex decision-making problems in a variety of domains, by optimizing reward signals obtained through interaction with an environment. However, many real-world scenarios involve multiple, potentially conflicting objectives that cannot be easily represented by a single scalar reward. Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (MORL) addresses this limitation by enabling agents to optimize several objectives simultaneously, explicitly reasoning about trade-offs between them. However, the ``black box" nature of the RL models makes the decision process behind chosen objective trade-offs unclear. Current Explainable Reinforcement Learning (XRL) methods are typically designed for single scalar rewards and do not account for explanations with respect to distinct objectives or user preferences. To address this gap, in this paper we propose TREX, a Trajectory based Explainability framework to explain Multi-objective Reinforcement Learning policies, based on trajectory attribution.
  TREX generates trajectories directly from the learned expert policy, across different user preferences and clusters them into semantically meaningful temporal segments. We quantify the influence of these behavioural segments on the Pareto trade-off by training complementary policies that exclude specific clusters, measuring the resulting relative deviation on the observed rewards and actions compared to the original expert policy. Experiments on multi-objective MuJoCo environments - HalfCheetah, Ant and Swimmer, demonstrate the framework's ability to isolate and quantify the specific behavioural patterns.
2603.21988.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）已證明其能夠解決各種領域中的複雜決策問題，通過優化與環境互動所獲得的獎勵信號。然而，許多現實場景涉及多個潛在衝突的目標，這些目標不能簡單地用單一的標量獎勵來表示。多目標強化學習（MORL）通過使代理能夠同時優化多個目標，明確考慮它們之間的權衡，來解決這一限制。然而，RL模型的“黑箱”特性使得所選擇的目標權衡背後的決策過程不明確。目前的可解釋強化學習（XRL）方法通常是為單一標量獎勵設計的，並未考慮與不同目標或用戶偏好相關的解釋。為了解決這一空白，在本文中，我們提出了TREX，一個基於軌跡的可解釋性框架，用於解釋多目標強化學習政策，基於軌跡歸因。<lb/>TREX直接從學習到的專家政策生成軌跡，跨越不同的用戶偏好並將其聚類為語義上有意義的時間段。我們通過訓練排除特定聚類的互補政策來量化這些行為段對帕累托權衡的影響，測量與原始專家政策相比，觀察到的獎勵和行動的相對偏差。對多目標MuJoCo環境——HalfCheetah、Ant和Swimmer的實驗，展示了該框架隔離和量化特定行為模式的能力。
2603.21988.author: Dilina Rajapakse et.al.
2603.21988.authors: Dilina Rajapakse, Juan C. Rosero, Ivana Dusparic
